title: Will the war in Iran advance or hinder Europe's energy transition?
subhead: European countries remain divided over how to respond to the surge in oil and gas prices. From electrification to subsidies for electric vehicles purchases and other measures, the solutions are well known, but require significant investment.

Pope denounces widening gap between the rich and poor during visit to Monaco
subhead: In an address in French from the balcony of the Prince's Palace on Saturday, Leo XIV denounced 'unjust configurations of power, structures of sin that dig chasms between poor and rich, between the privileged and the rejected, between friends and enemy.'

title: Revealed: Satellite image shows mysterious cargo at Iranian nuclear site in 2025
subhead: Le Monde reveals an image of a convoy at the gates of a major underground nuclear complex in Iran. According to several experts, it could be part of the country's stockpile of enriched uranium.

title: Matisse, the carver of light, at the Grand Palais in Paris
subhead: The delightful exhibition showcases later work by the artist who never stopped experimenting and features his famous cut-outs as well as monumental canvases.

title: Substack, a digital refuge for writers, accelerates its growth in France
subhead: Since 2017, the US platform has enabled users to publish content as monetizable newsletters, winning over many journalists and writers. The company recently appointed a manager to boost its growth in France.

In Nabatieh, southern Lebanon, 'no one ventures outside anymore apart from rescue teams'
subhead: Like 42 other rescue workers since the war began on March 2, 16-year-old Joude Souleiman was killed by an Israeli drone strike in Nabatieh, the second-largest Shiite city in southern Lebanon, where only a few hundred people out of 75,000 residents remain.

title: 'Trump would benefit from rereading Obama's 2015 speech on Iran'
subhead: The US president belatedly realized that the military option carried significant risks. He did not rely on his administration's recognized experts on Iran, notes Le Monde columnist Gilles Paris.

title: The US and Israel are fighting different wars against the same foe
subhead: Donald Trump and Benjamin Netanyahu share many objectives. However, their respective public opinions and electoral interests may ultimately highlight significant differences between the two leaders.
